questions.question: Define and explain Preorder traversal with an example tree showing the traversal order.
keyPoints: Preorder visits Node, Left, Right order
First node visited is always the root
Useful for creating tree copies, prefix expressions
Example with tree: root A, left B, right C produces A-B-C

questions.question: Explain Inorder traversal and state its important property for Binary Search Trees.
keyPoints: Inorder visits Left, Node, Right order
For BST, produces nodes in sorted ascending order
This property validates BST correctness
Also produces infix notation for expression trees

questions.question: Differentiate between Preorder, Inorder, and Postorder traversals with respect to when the root is visited.
keyPoints: Preorder: Root visited FIRST (N-L-R)
Inorder: Root visited MIDDLE (L-N-R)
Postorder: Root visited LAST (L-R-N)
Each serves different applications based on root position

questions.question: Explain Level Order traversal using a queue with algorithm steps.
keyPoints: Level order is Breadth-First traversal
Uses queue data structure for implementation
Processes all nodes at current level before next level
Root is processed first, children enqueued for future processing

questions.question: What is the time and space complexity of tree traversal algorithms? Explain.
keyPoints: Time complexity: O(n) for all traversals - each node visited once
Space complexity: O(h) for recursive, O(h) for iterative stack
h = height of tree: O(log n) for balanced, O(n) for skewed
Level order queue may need O(w) where w is maximum width

questions.question: Write an algorithm for recursive Inorder traversal of a binary tree.
keyPoints: Base case: if node is NULL, return
Recursively traverse left subtree
Visit (process) current node
Recursively traverse right subtree
L-NFollows-R pattern

questions.question: State two applications of each: Preorder, Inorder, and Postorder traversals.
keyPoints: Preorder: expression tree prefix notation, copy tree, directory listing
Inorder: BST sorted output, infix expression, validate BST property
Postorder: delete tree (free memory), postfix expression, dependency resolution

questions.question: Explain the three depth-first tree traversals (Preorder, Inorder, Postorder) with algorithms, example output, and applications.
keyPoints: Preorder (NLR): Visit node, traverse left, traverse right. Example: A-B-D-E-C-F. Applications: prefix expressions, copying trees
Inorder (LNR): Traverse left, visit node, traverse right. Example: D-B-E-A-C-F. Applications: BST sorted order, infix expressions
Postorder (LRN): Traverse left, traverse right, visit node. Example: D-E-B-F-C-A. Applications: delete trees, postfix expressions
All three use recursion with O(n) time and O(h) space

questions.question: Write C programs for recursive and iterative implementations of Inorder traversal explaining the use of stack.
keyPoints: Recursive: function calls itself for left, processes node, calls for right
Iterative: uses explicit stack to simulate recursion
Stack stores nodes while traversing left
When stack empty and current null, backtrack and process
Both achieve O(n) time, O(h) space for stack

questions.question: Explain Level Order traversal with algorithm and write pseudo-code. How does it differ from Depth-First traversals?
keyPoints: Level order uses queue not stack
Enqueue root, process, enqueue children while dequeuing
Processes level by level horizontally
DFS goes deep vertically before breadth
BFS/Level order: O(n) time, O(w) space for max width queue

questions.question: Given preorder: A-B-D-E-C-F and inorder: D-B-E-A-C-F, draw the binary tree and verify with postorder traversal.
keyPoints: Preorder first element A is root
Inorder splits: D-B-E is left subtree, C-F is right
From preorder, B is next root of left subtree
Continue splitting to reconstruct tree
Postorder should be D-E-B-F-C-A
